Short alt text: Layered HANA-X Copilot architecture from user intent through governed SAP execution.
Long description: A business user interacts through CopilotKit and AG-UI. LangGraph orchestrates intent refinement, policy, context, planning, and MCP tool execution. PostgreSQL is authoritative, Qdrant is a rebuildable retrieval index, LightRAG and preference memory are optional, Ollama supplies on-premises model inference, and released SAP interfaces connect to SAP S/4HANA. Identity, authorization, human approval, audit, observability, provenance, and retention span all layers.

Short alt text: Intent clarification flow with up to three targeted questions before plan confirmation and execution.
Long description: Natural-language intent is analyzed for completeness. Missing or ambiguous data triggers authorized open-text or multiple-choice clarification, with a default limit of three turns. Complete intent proceeds to a reviewable plan and policy-driven approval; unresolved intent ends in safe fallback, draft, escalation, or cancellation.

Short alt text: Knowledge and memory architecture using PostgreSQL, an outbox, Qdrant, and optional graph and preference memory.
Long description: Original evidence and normalized records are stored in PostgreSQL and governed file storage. A transactional outbox drives idempotent indexing into Qdrant and optional LightRAG. Context assembly combines authorized retrieval with optional curated preferences before LangGraph planning.

Short alt text: Governed ingestion pipeline for documents, email, URLs, and APIs.
Long description: Files, email, URLs, and API data pass validation, security and rights checks, immutable preservation, Docling or Crawl4AI normalization, source classification, PostgreSQL persistence, asynchronous indexing, retrieval quality checks, and controlled publication to the knowledge layer.

Short alt text: User-bound SAP identity and governed tool-execution flow.
Long description: An authenticated user request enters HANA-X Copilot, where policy and approval controls determine whether an MCP-adapted SAP tool may execute. The customer-selected identity propagation pattern reaches SAP Gateway and S/4HANA, which enforce SAP authorizations and return results with audit evidence.
